title: Handshake vs Reply.io — Honest Comparison for LinkedIn Automation
description: Comparing Handshake and Reply.io for LinkedIn outreach. See features, pricing, multichannel capabilities, and LinkedIn automation depth side-by-side.

overview.handshake: Handshake is a LinkedIn automation platform built from the ground up for multi-sender rotation. It lets teams manage unlimited LinkedIn sender accounts from a single dashboard, with built-in warmup scheduling, a unified inbox, and premium residential proxies included in every plan. Handshake's pricing model is based on the number of sender accounts — not per-seat — so your entire team can collaborate without multiplying costs. Plans start at $69/month for 1 sender and scale to enterprise volumes.
overview.competitor: Reply.io is a sales engagement platform that combines email outreach, LinkedIn automation, calls, SMS, and WhatsApp into multichannel sequences. Founded in 2014, Reply.io has evolved from an email-first tool into a comprehensive sales engagement suite. LinkedIn automation is available via a Chrome extension on the Professional plan at $89/month per user. Reply.io also offers AI-powered features including an AI SDR assistant, AI sequence generator, and smart reply suggestions. The platform includes a B2B contact database, email warmup, and CRM integrations.

featureComparison.handshake: Built-in, core feature — rotate outreach across multiple LinkedIn accounts automatically
featureComparison.competitor: Not available for LinkedIn — each user account runs LinkedIn independently
Email + LinkedIn + calls + SMS + WhatsApp in unified sequences

featureComparison.handshake: Cloud-based with dedicated residential proxies per sender
featureComparison.competitor: Chrome extension — runs in your browser, uses your local IP
AI SDR assistant, AI sequence writer, smart reply suggestions, intent detection

featureComparison.handshake: Single inbox for all LinkedIn sender accounts
featureComparison.competitor: Unified inbox across email channels — LinkedIn managed via extension

featureComparison.handshake: Not included — integrates with external data sources
featureComparison.competitor: Built-in database with 140M+ contacts, email finder, and enrichment
Email warmup included — no dedicated LinkedIn warmup

featureComparison.handshake: Unlimited team seats on every plan
featureComparison.competitor: Per-user pricing — $89/mo per user for Professional plan

featureComparison.handshake: Premium residential proxies included, human-like patterns, smart rate limiting
featureComparison.competitor: LinkedIn runs via Chrome extension on local IP — no dedicated proxies
Native integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive, plus Zapier and API

sections.handshakeWins: Handshake is dramatically stronger for dedicated LinkedIn automation. The cloud-based execution with residential proxies is fundamentally safer than Reply.io's Chrome extension approach. Multi-sender rotation lets teams distribute LinkedIn outreach across accounts automatically — Reply.io can't do this. The unified inbox for LinkedIn conversations gives teams centralized conversation management that Reply.io only provides for email. For pricing, 5 LinkedIn senders on Handshake cost $199/mo vs $445/mo on Reply.io — a 55% savings. And Handshake's LinkedIn-specific warmup ensures new accounts ramp safely, whereas Reply.io's warmup features are email-only.
sections.competitorWins: Reply.io is a full sales engagement suite where LinkedIn is one channel among many. If your team runs multichannel sequences spanning email, LinkedIn, calls, SMS, and WhatsApp, Reply.io provides that in a single platform. The AI features are genuinely useful — the AI SDR can generate entire sequences, suggest replies, and detect buying intent. The built-in B2B database eliminates the need for a separate data tool. CRM integrations with Salesforce and HubSpot are deep and bi-directional. For organizations that need a complete sales tech stack in one tool, Reply.io covers more ground.
sections.whoShouldChooseHandshake: Choose Handshake if LinkedIn is your primary outreach channel and you want purpose-built automation with multi-sender rotation. It's ideal for teams scaling LinkedIn beyond what one account can handle, agencies running client LinkedIn campaigns, and anyone who prioritizes LinkedIn account safety with cloud-based execution and residential proxies.
sections.whoShouldChooseCompetitor: Choose Reply.io if you need a complete sales engagement platform combining email, LinkedIn, phone, and SMS. It's the right choice for SDR teams who run multichannel cadences, organizations that want AI-powered sequence creation and optimization, and teams that need deep CRM integration with Salesforce or HubSpot.
sections.verdict: For LinkedIn-specific outreach at scale, Handshake is the clear winner — safer execution, multi-sender rotation, and significantly better team pricing. Reply.io is the better choice when you need a full multichannel sales engagement platform where LinkedIn is one component of a broader cadence. The decision comes down to whether LinkedIn is your primary channel (Handshake) or one of several (Reply.io).

faqs.question: Does Reply.io's Chrome extension put my LinkedIn account at risk?
faqs.answer: Chrome extensions are inherently riskier than cloud-based tools for LinkedIn automation. They inject scripts into LinkedIn's page and use your local IP address. Handshake's cloud-based approach with dedicated residential proxies provides a significantly safer alternative.

faqs.question: Can Reply.io do multi-sender rotation on LinkedIn?
faqs.answer: No. Each Reply.io user runs their LinkedIn automation independently through their own browser extension. There's no mechanism to distribute LinkedIn outreach across multiple sender accounts from a single campaign.

faqs.question: Is Reply.io worth it just for LinkedIn automation?
faqs.answer: Probably not. At $89/mo per user for LinkedIn access (Professional plan), Reply.io is expensive if you're only using the LinkedIn features. Handshake starts at $69/mo and is purpose-built for LinkedIn with better safety and multi-sender support.

faqs.question: Can I use both Handshake and Reply.io?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is a strong combination. Use Handshake for dedicated LinkedIn multi-sender outreach and Reply.io for email sequences and multichannel follow-ups. Connect them via CRM or API to maintain a unified prospect view.

faqs.question: Does Reply.io include LinkedIn warmup?
faqs.answer: No. Reply.io's warmup features are email-only (warming up email sending reputation). There's no LinkedIn-specific warmup for gradually ramping new accounts. Handshake includes automated LinkedIn warmup on all plans.
